qtype: percentage decrease, ratio division, fraction calculation
tex:
(a) A shop sells 58 televisions in one week.
\newline

The bar chart shows the number of televisions that the shop sells on five of the days.
\newline

(i) (b) Write down the number of televisions that the shop sells on Monday.
\newline

(c) Find the fraction of the televisions that the shop sells on Sunday.
\newline

(d) The number of televisions that the shop sells on the other two days is in the ratio

Wednesday : Friday = 2 : 3.
\newline

Complete the bar chart.
\newline

(e) Write down the mode.
\newline

(f) A television has a price of \$550.
\newline

This price is reduced by 4\%.
\newline

Calculate the new price of this television.
\newline

(g) The scatter diagram shows the prices of different sized televisions.
\newline

Write down the type of correlation shown in the scatter diagram.
\newline

(h) Hemang buys two televisions.
\newline

The probability that a television is faulty is 0.02.
\newline

(i) (i) Complete the tree diagram.
\newline

(j) Find the probability that Hemang buys two faulty televisions.
\newline

(k) The shop sells 4150 televisions in one year.
\newline

Calculate the expected number of faulty televisions.
\newline

\hline

\hline
\end{tabular}

tex:
(a) Town $S$ is 44 km from town $R$ on a bearing of $117^\circ$.
\newline
(i) (b) Using a scale of 1 cm represents 8 km, mark the position of town $S$.
\newline

(c) Anvi cycles the 44 km from $R$ to $S$.
\newline

She leaves $R$ at 13 15 and cycles at a speed of 12 km/h.
\newline

Work out the time she arrives at $S$.
\newline

(d) A tower has a height of 16 metres.
\newline

When Jai makes a scale drawing of the tower it has a height of 20 cm.
\newline

Work out the scale Jai uses, giving your answer in the form $1 : n$.
\newline

(e) $X$, $Y$ and $Z$ are three towns.
\newline

$X$ is on a bearing of $288^\circ$ from $Y$.
\newline

$Z$ is on a bearing of $018^\circ$ from $Y$.
\newline

(i) (f) Show that angle $XYZ$ is $90^\circ$.
\newline

(g) $XY = 6$ km and $YZ = 9.7$ km.
\newline

Calculate $XZ$.
\newline

qtype: substitution, solving linear equations, solving indices equation, solving simultaneous linear equations, listing integer solutions for inequalities
tex:
(a) $P = 3a + 5$

Find the value of $P$ when $a = 2$.
\newline

(b) Solve these equations.
\newline
(i) (c) $7x = -42$

(d) $9 (8x - 7) = 72$

(e) $5^8 \times 5^k = 5^{-24}$

Find the value of $k$.
\newline

(f) Solve the simultaneous equations.
\newline
\begin{align*}
-6x - y &= 13 \\
8x + y &= -51
\end{align*}

(g) $n$ is an integer where $n \geq -3$ and $n < 1$.
\newline

Write down all the possible values of $n$.
\newline

(h) A boy walks for 35 minutes at $x$ metres per minute.
\newline

He then runs for $t$ minutes at 160 metres per minute.
\newline

Write down an expression, in terms of $x$ and $t$, for the total distance, in metres, the boy travels.
\newline

(i) 
Find an expression for the area of this rectangle.
\newline

Give your answer in the form $x^2 + ax + b$.
\newline

qtype: calculating with fractions and percentages from given data
tex:
(a) 120 people teach in a university mathematics department.
\newline

Some information is shown in the table.
\newline
\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|}
\hline
& Lecturers & Professors & Total \\
\hline
Part-time & 11 &  &  \\
\hline
Full-time &  &  &  \\
\hline
Total &  &  & 120 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
One fifth of the people are professors.
\newline

30\% of the people are part-time.
\newline

Work out the number of full-time lecturers.
\newline

(b) $\mathscr{E} = \{\text{children in a school}\}$

$F = \{\text{children who like fruit}\}$

$V = \{\text{children who like vegetables}\}$

24 children like vegetables but do not like fruit.
\newline

8 children do not like fruit and do not like vegetables.
\newline

$n(F \cup V) = 9$ (Note: Text says $n(F+V)=9$, assuming Union or Intersection?)

$n(F) = 3 \times n(V)$

(i) (c) Complete the Venn diagram.
\newline

(d) Work out $n(F \cap V)$.
\newline

content.content:
120 people teach in a university mathematics department.
\newline
Some information is shown in the table.
\newline

One fifth of the people are professors.
\newline
30\% of the people are part-time.
\newline
Work out the number of full-time lecturers.
\newline

$\mathscr{E} = \{\text{children in a school}\}$
\newline
$F = \{\text{children who like fruit}\}$
\newline
$V = \{\text{children who like vegetables}\}$
\newline
24 children like vegetables but do not like fruit.
\newline
8 children do not like fruit and do not like vegetables.
\newline
$n(F \cup V) = 9$ (Note: Text says $n(F+V)=9$, assuming Union or Intersection?)
\newline
$n(F) = 3 \times n(V)$

Complete the Venn diagram.
\newline

Work out $n(F \cap V)$.
\newline

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item 120 people teach in a university mathematics department.
\newline
Some information is shown in the table.
\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|}
\hline
& Lecturers & Professors & Total \\
\hline
Part-time & 11 & ...... & ...... \\
\hline
Full-time & ...... & ...... & ...... \\
\hline
Total & ...... & ...... & 120 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{center}
One fifth of the people are professors.
\newline
30\% of the people are part-time.
\newline
Work out the number of full-time lecturers.

\AnswerLine
\Marks{4}

\item $\mathscr{E} = \{\text{children in a school}\}$
\newline
$F = \{\text{children who like fruit}\}$
\newline
$V = \{\text{children who like vegetables}\}$
\newline
24 children like vegetables but do not like fruit.
\newline
8 children do not like fruit and do not like vegetables.
\newline
$n(F \cup V) = 9$ (Note: Text says $n(F+V)=9$, assuming Union or Intersection?)
\newline
$n(F) = 3 \times n(V)$

\begin{subparts}
\item Complete the Venn diagram.
\relinput{Figures/Q08b.tex}

\Marks{3}

\item Work out $n(F \cap V)$.

\AnswerLine
\Marks{1}
\end{subparts}
\end{parts}
